title: Software Engineering
description:
**Meet the Team**
 The
 **Catalyst SD\\-WAN Control Plane** 
 team builds and maintains the software that keeps the
 **management and control layer** 
 of Cisco’s SD\\-WAN solution working end\\-to\\-end—secure sessions between WAN edge devices and the controllers/management plane, orchestration and discovery, certificate\\-backed identity, and the protocol and state\\-machine logic that must stay
 **reliable at scale** 
 across diverse customer networks. Prior SD\\-WAN experience is helpful but not required; we expect you to ramp quickly on our architecture and to bring depth in
 **large\\-scale networking software** 
 ,
 **security** 
 , and
 **operational rigor** 
 .
   

  

**Your Impact**
 You will
 **own complex, multi\\-component problems** 
 across the control plane—driving clarity from ambiguous requirements, producing designs that balance security, scale, and maintainability, and delivering through implementation, test strategy, and sustained production health. You will be a
 **go\\-to engineer** 
 for hard failures and cross\\-team integration, and you will
 **raise team quality** 
 through thorough design and code review, pragmatic mentoring, and improvements to diagnostics, tests, and tooling where they reduce customer pain.
   

  

**Minimum Qualifications**
* Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Electrical Engineering, or a related technical field plus 9 years of relevant experience, or Master’s degree in a related field plus 5 years of relevant experience.
* Expert\\-level software development in C/C\\+\\+ or comparable systems languages used in performance\\-sensitive networking software, including performance and memory discipline.
* Deep networking fundamentals applied to real systems: TCP/IP, routing and overlay concepts, TLS/DTLS, NAT traversal, and end\\-to\\-end troubleshooting across layers and teams.
* Repeated success shipping high\\-reliability, production\\-grade software in large codebases: strong testing posture, operational ownership, and sustained maintenance under customer load.
* Demonstrated ability to lead technical resolution for complex issues—debugging across components, coordinating with partner teams, and driving fixes to completion.


**Preferred Qualifications**
* Experience with WAN edge, VPN, or overlay networking at scale (IPsec/SSL VPN, DMVPN, MPLS L3VPN, EVPN, or similar), including failure modes seen in production.
* Strong grasp of distributed systems behavior under stress: connection churn, partial partitions, backoff policies, state recovery, and observability that supports real incident response.
* Hands\\-on strength in security engineering for networked systems: PKI lifecycle considerations, threat modeling for long\\-lived control channels, and secure\\-by\\-default configurations.
* Advanced Linux systems and networking debugging: concurrency, performance profiling, packet\\-path reasoning, and tooling for repeatable diagnosis.
* Track record of mentoring engineers and improving engineering systems (tests, CI signals, diagnostics, runbooks) in ways that outlast individual projects.


 Why Cisco?
   

  

 At Cisco, we’re revolutionizing how data and infrastructure connect and protect organizations in the AI era – and beyond. We’ve been innovating fearlessly for 40 years to create solutions that power how humans and technology work together across the physical and digital worlds. These solutions provide customers with unparalleled security, visibility, and insights across the entire digital footprint.
   

  

 Fueled by the depth and breadth of our technology, we experiment and create meaningful solutions. Add to that our worldwide network of doers and experts, and you’ll see that the opportunities to grow and build are limitless. We work as a team, collaborating with empathy to make really big things happen on a global scale. Because our solutions are everywhere, our impact is everywhere.
   

  

 We are Cisco, and our power starts with you.

title: Software Engineer III
description:
**About Crunchyroll**
 Founded by fans, Crunchyroll delivers the art and culture of anime to a passionate community. We super\\-serve over 100 million anime and manga fans across 200\\+ countries and territories, and help them connect with the stories and characters they crave. Whether that experience is online or in\\-person, streaming video, theatrical, games, merchandise, events and more, it’s powered by the anime content we all love.
   

  

 Join our team, and help us shape the future of anime!
   

  

**About The Role**
 We are seeking a Software Engineer who enjoys working at the intersection of front end engineering, product quality, and large scale platform development. This role sits within our Fan Experience Engineering organization and focuses on building the systems and tools that help us deliver a fast, stable, and consistent experience across a wide range of devices.
   

  

 You will write production ready code, contribute to shared architecture, and build automated systems that help teams ship with confidence. The work focuses on applying front end engineering skills to build the test systems, tooling, and infrastructure that keep our user experiences reliable and high\\-performing for millions of fans.
   

  

 If you enjoy solving complex technical problems and improving both product and platform reliability, you will find this role rewarding.
   

  

**What you will be doing:**
* Tackle a uniquely complex device ecosystem. Crunchyroll reaches fans on a large range of TVs, consoles, and partner platforms—each with its own architecture and challenges. You’ll design automation that delivers a consistently exceptional experience across this diverse landscape.
* Engineer for scale and reliability. Build robust, intelligent automated test systems and tools that power fast, reliable releases for millions of users around the world.
* Collaborate across disciplines. Partner with engineers, product teams, and external partners to integrate, validate, and optimize the Crunchyroll experience on every device.
* Shape the future of quality. Influence technical direction and champion a culture of excellence in code health, test strategy, and automation—leading by example, not by process.
* Empower the community. Share insights, tools, and frameworks that elevate the entire engineering ecosystem and help fans everywhere enjoy anime the way it’s meant to be experienced.


**Qualifications**
* Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field, and/or 5\\+ years of practical experience
* Strong professional experience with JavaScript, TypeScript, React, and modern front end technologies.
* Solid understanding of HTML, CSS, responsive design, and browser performance characteristics.
* Experience working across different device platforms, such as smart TVs and game consoles, is a strong plus.


**Curiosity, creativity, and a continuous improvement mindset.**
* Hands\\-on experience developing automation for consumer electronics or connected devices across varied hardware and operating systems.
* Excellent problem\\-solving and communication skills and the ability to collaborate effectively with other engineers, product managers and program managers.
* Empathy for both internal teams and external partners.


**About The Team**
 The Fan Experience Engineering team focuses on building the products and infrastructure that support the Crunchyroll experience across web, mobile, and living room devices. We partner with platform engineering, content operations, payments, distribution teams, and external partners to improve quality, enable new experiences, and expand our reach. The work directly affects how fans discover, watch, and enjoy anime every day.
   

  

**About Our Values**
 We want to be everything for someone rather than something for everyone and we do this by living and modeling our values in all that we do. We value
   

  

* Courage. We believe that when we overcome fear, we enable our best selves.
* Curiosity. We are curious, which is the gateway to empathy, inclusion, and understanding.
* Kaizen. We have a growth mindset committed to constant forward progress.
* Service. We serve our community with humility, enabling joy and belonging for others.


 Our commitment to diversity and inclusion
   

  

 Our mission of helping people belong reflects our commitment to diversity \\& inclusion. It's just the way we do business.
   

  

 We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at Crunchyroll. Pursuant to applicable law, we do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.
   

  

 Crunchyroll, LLC is an independently operated joint venture between US\\-based Sony Pictures Entertainment, and Japan's Aniplex, a subsidiary of Sony Music Entertainment (Japan) Inc., both subsidiaries of Tokyo\\-based Sony Group Corporation.

title: Lead Software engineer (Java Full stack Developer)
description:
**Our Purpose**
*Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200\\+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential.*
**Title And Summary**
 Lead Software engineer (Java Full stack Developer)
   

  

**All About Us**
 MasterCard is a technology company in the global payments business. We connect consumers, financial institutions, merchants, governments, and businesses worldwide and enable them to use secure and convenient electronic forms of payment.
   

  

 Join the industry’s most passionate, motivated \\& engaged global team \\- Our employees are encouraged to drive innovation every day in support of a more connected world – A World Beyond Cash.
   

  

**Overview**
 The MasterCard B\\&MI Technology development team is engaged in working with new and innovative technologies to build business solutions that keep Mastercard positioned as a leader in delivering value added business analytic and reporting solutions to our diverse customer base. We work collaboratively with our product partners and other technical teams to continuously improve and enhance our existing products and drive new products to the global marketplace. We are currently seeking candidates that will be responsible to propose, design scalable solutions for our products.
   

  

 Role :\\-
   

  

* Design secure, reliable, and scalable solutions for globally distributed customer facing products.
* Lead/mentor junior team members in learning new development skills and lead overall delivery/feature execution as per roadmap.
* Support development teams and work with stakeholders, promoting agile development

* Define, plan, and support execution of the technology strategy for Operational insights products
* Engage with wider Mastercard Architecture \\& Technology teams to ensure alignment on the wider technical strategies and policies within Mastercard
* Research, create and evaluate technical solution alternatives for the business needs using current and upcoming technologies and frameworks


 Technologies: \\-
   

  

* Java, J2EE, micro services, RESTful APIs, Angular, Web Services, JavaScript, Docker
* Framework \\- Spring (Spring Boot, Spring Data, Spring Batch, Spring REST, Spring Cloud, AOP), Hibernate
* UI/UX frameworks and best practices (React, Angular)
* API tooling and design best practices
* Cloud and DevOps Architecture, especially via Pivotal Cloud Foundry
* Databases – Oracle, SQL server, PostgreSQL, Cassandra, ElasticSearch
* Tools \\- Eclipse or IntelliJ, Sonar, GIT, Maven, SQL Developer, Jenkins, CI/CD, Gatling


**All About You**
 The ideal candidate has an enthusiasm for finding scalable solutions to complex problems and identifying process improvements to increase efficiency.
   

  

* Bachelor's degree in Information Technology, Computer Science or Management Information Systems and equivalent work experience
* Thorough knowledge and experience applying software engineering concepts and methodologies
* A high degree of initiative and self\\-motivation with a willingness and ability to learn and take on challenging opportunities
* Experience designing, developing, and deploying Java webservices using a mix of relational and non\\-relational persistence
* Strong communication skills to effectively communicate complicated subjects to both technical and non\\-technical audiences
* Experience and knowledgeable in DWH concepts, large data processing tools etc.


 Beneficial Experience: \\-
   

  

* Familiarity with the payments industry, payment processing, reporting and data \\& analytics domain
* Experience designing for standard and regulatory compliance (e.g., PCI\\-DSS, GDPR, etc.)

* Exposure to coding agents GitHub Copilot, Cursor, Claude Code to the list and agentic stack\\- crewAi, n8n etc and QA automation frameworks like Selenium , Microsoft Playwright is a big plus


**Corporate Security Responsibility**
 All activities involving access to Mastercard assets, information, and networks comes with an inherent risk to the organization and, therefore, it is expected that every person working for, or on behalf of, Mastercard is responsible for information security and must:
   

  

* Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices;
* Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed;
* Report any suspected information security violation or breach, and
* Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.
